title: OSC 133 — Semantic Prompts (FinalTerm / FTCS shell integration)
summary: OSC 133 marks shell prompt, command-input, and command-output boundaries so terminals can navigate by command, fold output, and color the prompt by exit status. Four subcommands: A (prompt start), B (command-line start), C (output start), D[;exitcode] (command finished). Originated in the defunct FinalTerm emulator; harmonized by Per Bothner's Semantic Prompts proposal; near-universally adopted.

attribution.note: Semantic Prompts proposal is the harmonizing cross-terminal spec; defines purpose of prompt/input/output marking.
OSC 133 ; A ST = FTCS_PROMPT, start of prompt. Stated identically by Windows Terminal docs and Contour.
Second independent source for A semantics (quorum).
OSC 133 ; B ST = FTCS_COMMAND_START, start of command line / end of prompt.
Second independent source for B semantics (quorum).
OSC 133 ; C ST = FTCS_COMMAND_EXECUTED, start of command output / end of command line.
Second independent source for C semantics (quorum).
OSC 133 ; D [ ; exitcode ] ST = FTCS_COMMAND_FINISHED; 0 success, nonzero error; omitting exitcode leaves the mark default-colored.
Second independent source for D semantics including optional exit code (quorum).
iTerm2 states the sequences were pioneered by the now-defunct FinalTerm emulator and documents them.
OSC frame is ESC ] ... terminated by ST (ESC \\); xterm convention also accepts BEL (0x07) as terminator.
OSC = ESC ] (0x9D in 8-bit). ECMA-48 5th ed. 1991 defines the OSC frame and ST; command numbers are not registered anywhere.

frame.note: Per ECMA-48 5th ed. 1991 / ISO 6429. Command numbers (the '133') have no registry; see terminfo.dev and xterm ctlseqs.

terminator_detail.note: ECMA-48 mandates ST. xterm and virtually all modern terminals ALSO accept BEL on the parsing side. Emit ST for spec-correctness; accept both when parsing.

params.name: FTCS_PROMPT
params.meaning: Start of the prompt. Emitted by the shell precmd hook just before printing PS1.

params.name: FTCS_COMMAND_START
params.meaning: Start of the command line / end of the prompt. Everything after this until C is what the user typed.

params.name: FTCS_COMMAND_EXECUTED
params.meaning: Start of command output / end of the command line. Emitted from the shell preexec hook, just before the command runs.

params.name: FTCS_COMMAND_FINISHED
params.meaning: End of the command. Optional exit code as a second parameter: 0 = success, nonzero = error. Omitting the exit code leaves the mark default-colored (no success/error tint).

subparams.name: ExitCode
subparams.meaning: Optional integer exit status of the finished command. 0 = success (often green mark), nonzero = error (often red mark). When absent, the terminal does not tint the mark.

variants.label: OSC 633 (VS Code)
variants.note: VS Code's integrated terminal uses OSC 633 as a superset/sibling of OSC 133 (633;A/B/C/D mirror 133, plus 633;E command line, 633;P properties like Cwd). VS Code emits BOTH 133 and 633 for portability. Treat 633 as a distinct entry; cross-linked via see_also.

variants.label: FTCS_COMMAND (P;)
variants.note: Some implementations add OSC 133 ; P ; key=value ST for aux properties (e.g. Cwd, prompt kind) and OSC 133 ; L for fresh-line. These are extensions in the Semantic Prompts proposal beyond the core A/B/C/D and support varies; treat as partial.

support_matrix.notes: Origin of the FTCS sequences; project is defunct but documented by iTerm2.
Documents the sequences; also defines its own OSC 1337 family for additional shell-integration features.
Recognizes OSC 133 A/B/C/D for prompt/command tracking.
Implements semantic zones from OSC 133; documents shell integration.
Uses OSC 133 for shell integration / command tracking.
Supports OSC 133 shell integration out of the box.
Documents A/B/C/D identically to Contour; supports D exit-code coloring.
Dedicated OSC-133 shell-integration page; authoritative cross-source for A/B/C/D.
Prefers OSC 633 (superset) but also accepts/emits OSC 133 for portability.
Block model derives from prompt/command boundaries; uses shell-integration marks though its native model predates a pure 133 dependency.
Reference implementation behind the Semantic Prompts proposal.

gotchas: Terminator divergence: ECMA-48 requires ST (ESC \, i.e. \x1b\x5c). xterm and nearly every modern terminal ALSO accept BEL (\x07). Emit ST; accept both when parsing. Mixing a bare ESC followed by an unrelated byte will mis-terminate the string.

Wrap marks in PROMPT_COMMAND/precmd, not inside the visible PS1 width calculation: in bash/zsh the mark bytes must be wrapped in \[ \] (bash) or %{ %} (zsh) so the shell does not count them as visible columns, otherwise line editing and wrapping break.
C must be emitted from preexec (just before the command runs), not from precmd, or output folding will include the command line itself.
D's exit code is OPTIONAL; if you always want success/error coloring you must pass it explicitly (\x1b]133;D;$?\x1b\). A bare D leaves the mark untinted.
OSC 633 (VS Code) is NOT the same entry: 633 is a superset with extra subcommands (E command-line, P properties). Do not assume a 133 parser handles 633;P key=value.
There is NO registry for the number 133 itself; collisions are possible in principle but 133 is de-facto reserved for FinalTerm/FTCS across the ecosystem.

8-bit forms (0x9D for OSC, 0x9C for ST) exist per ECMA-48 but are rarely used in UTF-8 locales because they collide with UTF-8 continuation bytes; always prefer the 7-bit ESC-based forms in modern pipelines.

v1_smoke_test.asserts: Entry emits a syntactically correct ST-terminated example (\x1b]133;A\x1b\\) and records that BEL (\x07) is also accepted on the parsing side, capturing the ECMA-48-vs-practice divergence.
v1_smoke_test.behavioral_conformance: deferred to v2 (headless VTE harness asserting observed prompt/command/output zones); support_matrix cells are pre-shaped to receive those results.
